common_rules.rights_floor: no reduction may violate constitutional rights, due process, treaty or tribal obligations, or vested legal commitments
common_rules.continuity_floor: no abrupt interruption of active medical treatment, disability support, life-safety operations, or executed contracts
common_rules.score_request: annual 2026-2036 federal savings, cost shifts, implementation, service, access, workforce, regional, macroeconomic, interest, deficit, and debt effects

instruments.specification: reduce Medicare Advantage benchmarks uniformly by 10 percent beginning in 2027
use CBO's selected nondefense discretionary option only after program-level schedule and floors are enumerated

service_floors: Medicare Advantage county network adequacy and plan-exit monitoring
enrollee notice, special enrollment, continuity of active treatment, and appeal
enumerated nondefense programs rather than an undisclosed across-the-board reduction
life-safety, justice, inspection, weather, and core statistical continuity
variants.principal_exposure: Medicare Advantage plans and enrollees plus workers, grantees, and users of the selected nondefense programs

instruments.specification: CBO funding schedule reducing defense budget authority by $1.118T and outlays by $959B over 2025-2034, replaced before scoring with a capability-level 2027-start schedule
reduce Medicare Advantage benchmarks uniformly by 10 percent beginning in 2027

service_floors: defense strategy, force, munitions, readiness, nuclear command-and-control, cyber, treaty, and personnel transition review
no across-the-board defense sequester presented as a capability plan
Medicare Advantage network, quality, active-treatment continuity, county exit, and appeal protections
variants.principal_exposure: military personnel, contractors, defense communities and capabilities, Medicare Advantage plans, providers, and enrollees

instruments.specification: same CBO defense funding schedule, replaced before scoring with a capability-level 2027-start schedule
reduce Medicare Advantage benchmarks uniformly by 10 percent beginning in 2027
federal per-enrollee caps for all Medicaid eligibility categories using CPI-U growth, with the protection modifications below
instruments.source: CBO 60896 option range; exact variant score confirmation required

instruments.specification: highest CBO reduce-hospital-outpatient-payment alternative, with rural and access-critical exceptions below
instruments.source: CBO compendium; exact payment classes required
CBO compendium; exact subsidy parameters required

instruments.specification: CBO reduce-certain-state-and-local-grants option after every grant line is enumerated

service_floors: all broad-shared defense and Medicare protections
Medicaid enrollment-responsive caps rather than aggregate block grants
automatic recession, epidemic, disaster, enrollment, and high-cost case adjustments
eligibility, mandatory benefit, disability, long-term-care, provider-access, and due-process floors
rural, safety-net, teaching, and access-critical hospital review
enumerated grant and crop-insurance changes with state, farm, disaster, and food-system effects
variants.principal_exposure: states, Medicaid enrollees and providers, Medicare Advantage enrollees and plans, hospitals, defense communities, farmers, and state and local grant recipients

arithmetic_warning: Published individual estimates use 2025-2034 and are not additive. Service floors and a 2027 start materially alter the options and require a new joint score.
